description: Eastman Kodak invented the digital camera but failed to effectively pivot its business model away from high-margin film toward digital photography during the 2000s. The internal resistance and lack of conviction led to a slow decline in both market share and public perception of innovation.

key_events.description: Kodak releases EasyShare cameras but keeps focus on film printing.
Competitors like Canon and Sony capture significant market share in the digital space.
Kodak announces major layoffs and factory closures.
Kodak files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling long-term decay of reputation due to hesitation in market disruption.
